description: Overview of the fundamental concepts relating training to sport: warm-up, cool-down, sport-specific training, youth development, and injury prevention.

questions.text: What is the purpose of a warm-up before training?
questions.explanation: The warm-up gradually prepares the body for exercise: it raises body temperature, increases blood flow to the muscles, and reduces the risk of injury.

options.text: To accelerate the onset of fatigue
To gradually prepare the body for exercise
To directly improve competition results
To shorten the training session

questions.text: The cool-down is the closing phase of training that supports regeneration.
questions.explanation: True. The cool-down gradually reduces exercise intensity, aids in lactate removal, and speeds up recovery.

options.text: True
False

questions.text: What does sport-specificity mean in training?
questions.explanation: Sport-specificity means that the content and methods of training are adapted to the demands of the given sport, so that development is directly linked to match situations.

options.text: Applying identical training methods in every sport
Adapting training content to the requirements of the given sport
Performing only general conditioning work
Applying the most intense training method available

questions.text: What is the primary goal of youth development in sport?
questions.explanation: The primary goal of youth development is the long-term development of young athletes, encompassing the laying of technical, physical, and mental foundations.

options.text: To achieve immediate competition results
The long-term development of young athletes
The immediate application of adult training methods
To complete as many training sessions as possible

questions.text: Injury prevention is an integral part of the training plan.
questions.explanation: True. Deliberate training planning — including appropriate load-rest ratios, warm-up, and stretching — is the foundation of injury prevention.

options.text: True
False

questions.text: Which combination of abilities best characterises football?
questions.explanation: Football is a complex sport in which aerobic endurance, speed, strength, and coordination all play a decisive role — no single ability dominates.

options.text: Speed exclusively
A combination of abilities: endurance, speed, strength, and coordination
Endurance exclusively
Strength exclusively

questions.text: What is the difference between general and specific training?
questions.explanation: General training develops basic physical abilities (e.g., running, strength), while specific training is directly linked to the movements of the sport (e.g., ball exercises in football).

options.text: There is no difference between them
General training develops basic abilities; specific training develops sport-specific movements
Specific training is always more intense
General training is only for adults

questions.text: Young athletes should train at the same intensity as adult athletes.
questions.explanation: False. The developing body of young athletes requires different loading; youth training is built on age-appropriate, gradual load progression.

options.text: True
False

questions.text: What is the relationship between training and competition?
questions.explanation: Training is the means for achieving competition performance: the planned loads and development work are aligned with the demands of match situations.

options.text: Competition and training are entirely independent of each other
Training prepares the athlete to achieve competition performance
Competition replaces training
The goal of training is to avoid competition

questions.text: Dynamic stretching is recommended for warm-up and static stretching for cool-down.
questions.explanation: True. Before training, dynamic (movement-based) stretching prepares the muscles; during cool-down, static (held positions) stretching aids regeneration.

options.text: True
False
